calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-13-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg (Petitioner) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Michael Freedman appeared by zoom for William Nestle (Objector).

The court stated that it is very confused by Ms. Friedeberg's petition and asked how it ended up here. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she represented Matthew in a conservatorship for the grandmother, that the fees for services that were provided prior to the grandmother's death are still outstanding and that she was told she needed to file a creditor's claim in order to preserve her right to be paid. Ms. Friedeberg also stated that she does not want to be the administrator and that she asked Barbara Simon to be the administrator, but Ms. Simon does not want to be on one side over the other. The court asked if it can be filed in the trust. Mr. Freedman stated that this case settled, that all of the parties agreed that the various probate petitions would go off calendar and they were, and that after the settlement, Ms. Friedeberg's client decided he wanted to be paid for his attorney's fees. Mr. Freedman also stated that there is nothing in the settlement to allow Matthew to come back later and get fees or to re-file a probate petition, and that it is contrary to the settlement agreement. The court raised the issue of two wills being submitted. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the conservatorship was established for the benefit of Ms. Nestel, that the code is clear that it is legal for the conservator to petition for fees and that she does not care which will is admitted. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is denied.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: S. Park. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Continued To Dec-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for Matthew Nestel (petitioner).
Michael Freedman appeared by zoom for William Nestel (Petitioner/Contestant).

The Court stated that this matter appears to still have notice issues and that it still does not have objections to William's petition. Mr. Freedman stated that there is a trust matter that is related to this probate matter, that they have had discussions in the last several weeks, that they are still talking and that they have mediation set for this Thursday. Mr. Freedman also stated that they are still moving towards settlement, requested 60-days to wrap up the case and stated that if the matter doesn't settle, he will go back and address the notice issue. The Court stated that it is not sure if they had a meeting of the minds as far as who needed to be served. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she is in agreement with Mr. Freedman, Mr. Freedman stated that they have personal service on Matthew and Rachael, and that they intend to do personal service on the others. The Court informed Counsel that the pending petitions will be continued to the same date to be heard by the same Judge for judicial economy. Ms. Friedeberg stated that there is a matter on calendar for November 2, 2020 for Nancy Lewellen's accounting and that she prefers that the matter not be moved because that's maybe what is holding up the settlement. Mr. Freedman stated that if the case settles, there is a petition for instructions that is also on calendar for November 2, 2020 and that they have a need for a distribution. The Court informed the Parties that the November 2nd matters will not be heard and that if they want to advance anything, they will need to come in ex parte. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed, and the Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary are continued to December 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Settlement of First Account and Report of Trustee, etc., the Petition for Instructions and the Petition to Bring the Trust Under Court Supervision on calendar for November 2, 2020 in the trust case (PTR-19-303036) are also continued to December 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Ideta. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Continued To Oct-20-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Continued To Oct-20-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for Matthew Nestel.
Michael Freedman appeared by telephone for William Nestel.

The Court questions where the parties intend to go and stated that the will contest needs service of the summons. Mr. Freedman stated that he is in the process of completing the proof of service, that the 2018 will that is being offered is an exercise of power of appointment over the trust and that the only parties that need to be served as far as he can tell are Rachel Nestel and Barbara Simon. Mr. Freedman also stated that if service is required on all parties, service will take longer. The Court stated that a fundamental of probate is that notice be provided to any and all parties that are entitled to notice, and suggested that Mr. Freedman take a broad view. Ms. Friedeberg stated that she will be filing an objection to William's probate petition. The Court stated that its expectation is that everything is teed up, that proper service including service of the summons is done and that objections are in writing. The Court also advised the parties to think about discovery. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to October 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
